problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
mechanism: Small molecule inhibitor of enzyme active site
hypothesis: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Small. Small molecule inhibitor of enzyme active site directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Randomized controlled trial Phase I with 8 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.
